commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the current PR is a viable candidate with no narrow automated blocker found.; Cleared: The diff changes local session cleanup/reporting code and tests only, with no dependency, CI, credential, package, or network execution surface introduced. (sha=c079b74d630a48643bc68dc1f630bade32ef09b4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds deleted/reset archive cleanup reporting to `openclaw sessions cleanup` dry-run/apply summaries, threads archive cleanup counts through session maintenance, and adds regression tests for dry-run, reset retention, and disk-budget double-counting.

PR surface: Source +105, Tests +250. Total +355 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.10 dry-run cleanup report unreferenced artifacts and disk-budget removals but not stale deleted/reset archive cleanup. I did not run a live CLI repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the current PR is a viable candidate with no narrow automated blocker found.

target.title: [codex] fix(sessions): report archive cleanup in sessions cleanup
target.body:
Fixes #75658

## What Problem This Solves

`openclaw sessions cleanup --dry-run` did not preview stale `.deleted.*` and `.reset.*` archive files, and the apply summary did not report when those physical archive files were removed. Operators could see only unreferenced-artifact counts even when deleted/reset session archives were the files consuming disk.

## Why This Change Was Made

The cleanup command already had an archive retention helper, but dry-run did not call it and store maintenance discarded its removal count. This change reuses the existing archive-retention rules for both preview and apply reporting instead of adding a second cleanup policy.

Reviewer follow-up: dry-run archive cleanup now accepts an exclusion set, and the sessions cleanup preview passes in the disk-budget simulated removal paths. A stale archive already claimed by disk-budget cleanup is therefore not counted again as deleted/reset archive cleanup.

## User Impact

Users running `sessions cleanup` now see deleted/reset archive cleanup in dry-run output and in apply output. Forced cleanup also scans the store directory for stale deleted archives even when no session rows were removed, while `resetArchiveRetention: false` still preserves reset archives.

## Evidence

Focused validation:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts src/commands/sessions-cleanup.test.ts src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.archive-cleanup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-session-accessor-boundary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/store.pruning.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Real CLI proof, with temp paths redacted:

```text
$ node --import tsx src/entry.ts --no-color sessions cleanup --store "$TMP/archive-store/sessions.json" --dry-run --enforce
Session store: $TMP/archive-store/sessions.json
Maintenance mode: enforce
Entries: 0 -> 0 (remove 0)
Would prune missing transcripts: 0
Would retire stale direct DM sessions: 0
Would prune stale model-run probes: 0
Would prune stale: 0
Would cap overflow: 0
Would prune deleted/reset archives: 2
Would prune unreferenced artifacts: 0

after dry-run files:
["fresh.jsonl.deleted.2026-06-26T00-00-00.000Z","redacted-deleted.jsonl.deleted.2026-01-01T00-00-00.000Z","redacted-reset.jsonl.reset.2026-01-01T00-00-00.000Z","sessions.json"]

$ node --import tsx src/entry.ts --no-color sessions cleanup --store "$TMP/archive-store/sessions.json" --enforce
Session store: $TMP/archive-store/sessions.json
Applied maintenance. Current entries: 0
Pruned deleted/reset archives: 2

after apply files:
["fresh.jsonl.deleted.2026-06-26T00-00-00.000Z","sessions.json"]
```

Disk-budget double-count proof from the same CLI command in JSON mode, with `maxDiskBytes: 1000` and one oversized stale archive:

```json
{
  "archiveCleanup": {
    "scannedFiles": 0,
    "removedFiles": 0
  },
  "diskBudget": {
    "removedFiles": 1,
    "totalBytesBefore": 2085,
    "totalBytesAfter": 85
  }
}
```

Not run:

- `codex review --base origin/main` was attempted, but the local approval policy rejected the command because it would export the unpublished diff/repository context to an external service.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to inspect the issue, avoid duplicate PRs, implement the patch, and run the focused validation above.
target.author: samrusani
